attractions.name: Castner Glacier Ice Cave
attractions.aka: Castner Creek ice cave, Richardson Hwy Mile 217
attractions.season: Cave safest/most solid late winter; creek-bed walk passable Jun-Sep
attractions.blurb: Blue ice cave at the snout of Castner Glacier, 1.3 mi up a gravel creek bed from a Richardson Highway pullout at Mile 217.
attractions.tip: Go Feb-Mar when the creek is frozen flat and the roof is solid; summer meltwater makes the cave dangerous and the walk muddy.
attractions.duration_note: half-day from Delta Junction or Fairbanks

attractions.name: Ruth Glacier & the Great Gorge
attractions.aka: Don Sheldon Amphitheater, Ruth Gorge
attractions.season: Ski-equipped glacier landings roughly May-early Sep, weather permitting
attractions.blurb: Mile-wide glacier trench walled by 5,000-ft granite; the standard Denali flightseeing ski landing and a world climbing objective.
attractions.tip: Pay for the glacier-landing option, not the plain flyover - about $150 extra buys 20 min standing on ice below Moose's Tooth.
attractions.duration_note: 3 hr from Talkeetna including weather hold

attractions.name: Kahiltna Glacier Base Camp
attractions.aka: Denali Base Camp, Kahiltna International, 7,200 ft
attractions.season: Camp staffed and airstrip active late Apr-mid Jul
attractions.blurb: Seasonal ski-plane airstrip on the Southeast Fork Kahiltna where every West Buttress climb of Denali starts and ends.
attractions.tip: Non-climbers can land here on a Talkeetna flightseeing trip in May-June while camp is staffed; by late July it is gone.

attractions.name: Bear Glacier Lagoon
attractions.aka: Bear Glacier, Kenai Fjords iceberg lagoon
attractions.season: Jun-early Sep; boat access depends on surf over the outlet bar
attractions.blurb: Kenai Fjords' largest glacier ends in a 5-mile iceberg lagoon; access is by helicopter or a surf-landing water taxi.
attractions.tip: Heli-kayak trips from Seward run about $700 and overfly Resurrection Bay; boat trips get cancelled when the bar breaks.

attractions.name: Alsek River Raft Expedition
attractions.aka: Alsek Lake, Dry Bay takeout, Tatshenshini-Alsek
attractions.season: Guided launches Jun-Aug only
attractions.blurb: Ten-to-twelve-day raft expedition out of the Yukon through St. Elias icefields to Alsek Lake's icebergs and a bush-plane takeout.
attractions.tip: Glacier Bay NP river permits are lottery-allocated in February; nearly everyone goes with one of the few permitted outfitters.
attractions.duration_note: 10-12 days; fly out of Dry Bay to Yakutat

attractions.name: Kongakut River Float
attractions.aka: Arctic Refuge packraft trip, Caribou Pass
attractions.season: Mid-Jun-late Jul only, between breakup and freeze-up
attractions.blurb: The classic Arctic Refuge float: raft or packraft down the Brooks Range to the coastal plain, timed to the Porcupine caribou herd.
attractions.tip: Wheel-plane charter from Fairbanks via Arctic Village; late June hits the caribou migration, July is warmer but far buggier.
attractions.duration_note: 8-10 days plus weather days for air charters

attractions.name: Noatak River Float
attractions.aka: Lake Matcharak put-in, Grand Canyon of the Noatak
attractions.season: Late Jun-Aug after ice-out
attractions.blurb: Gentle 400-mile wilderness river draining Gates of the Arctic, with float-plane put-ins at Lake Matcharak or Twelvemile Creek.
attractions.tip: Class I water makes it the friendliest Arctic float, but budget two weather days at each end for the Bettles air charter.

attractions.name: Six Mile Creek Whitewater
attractions.aka: Sixmile Creek, Hope Highway
attractions.season: Runnable Jun-early Sep on glacial melt
attractions.blurb: Three canyons of Class IV-V glacial whitewater near Hope, the hardest commercially run river on the Kenai Peninsula.
attractions.tip: The third canyon requires passing a swim test; drysuits are provided. Trips launch off the Hope Highway, 90 min from Anchorage.
attractions.duration_note: half-day; easy day trip from Anchorage

attractions.name: Ketchikan Harbor Seaplane Base
attractions.aka: Ketchikan floatplane dock, Tongass Narrows
attractions.season: Year-round; best visibility May-Sep
attractions.blurb: Downtown floatplane dock on the Tongass Narrows where Misty Fjords flightseeing and remote Tongass cabin charters take off.
attractions.tip: Flights cancel often in rain - book Misty Fjords for the first hours in port so you keep a weather backup slot later.

attractions.name: Godwin Glacier Dog Sled Camp
attractions.aka: Seward glacier dog sledding
attractions.season: Glacier camp operates late May-early Sep
attractions.blurb: Helicopter lands you on a glacier above Seward for a real summer sled run behind a working Iditarod team on snow.
attractions.tip: Weather cancels flights often; take the earliest slot of the day and keep the afternoon free as a rebooking window.

attractions.name: Seavey's IdidaRide Sled Dog Tours
attractions.aka: Seavey kennel, Old Exit Glacier Road
attractions.season: Summer cart tours mid-May-mid Sep
attractions.blurb: Iditarod-champion family kennel outside Seward: summer wheeled-cart rides, puppy time and a race-gear talk from real mushers.
attractions.tip: Cheapest way to meet a working Iditarod team without a helicopter; it is 2 mi up Old Exit Glacier Rd, so pair with Exit Glacier.

attractions.name: Aialik Bay Sea Kayak Drop-off
attractions.aka: Aialik Glacier kayaking, Kenai Fjords
attractions.season: Jun-early Sep; water taxis stop running mid-Sep
attractions.blurb: Water taxi drops paddlers on a beach across from calving Aialik Glacier for day or multi-day kayaking inside Kenai Fjords NP.
attractions.tip: Guided day trips include the 1.5-hr boat each way; there are no cabins in the bay, so overnights mean a tent and a bear can.

attractions.name: Blackstone Bay Kayak Drop-off
attractions.aka: Blackstone Glacier, Whittier kayaking
attractions.season: Jun-early Sep
attractions.blurb: Whittier's short-haul fjord: three tidewater glaciers and a wall of waterfalls, 45 min by boat with no open-water crossing.
attractions.tip: Better value than the big glacier cruise if you paddle - drop-offs leave Whittier around 9am, ahead of the cruise-boat wake.
attractions.duration_note: full day from Anchorage via the Whittier tunnel

attractions.name: Shoup Bay State Marine Park
attractions.aka: Shoup Glacier, Valdez kayak cabins
attractions.season: Paddling Jun-Sep; cabins bookable year-round
attractions.blurb: Tidewater glacier, huge kittiwake colony and three bookable state-park cabins about 8 mi by kayak or trail west of Valdez.
attractions.tip: Cabins open on Alaska State Parks reservations six months out; the lagoon entrance is tide-gated, so paddle it near slack water.

attractions.name: Tolovana Hot Springs
attractions.aka: Tolovana cabins, Elliott Highway trail
attractions.season: Year-round; easiest on snow Feb-Mar, boggy in summer
attractions.blurb: Three cedar soaking tubs and three rental cabins on a ridge north of Fairbanks, reached by an 11-mile trail off the Elliott Hwy.
attractions.tip: Rent a cabin - the tubs come with it. Winter access is ski, fat bike or snowmachine, and the return has one brutal climb.
attractions.duration_note: overnight minimum; 11 mi in each way

attractions.name: Chief Shakes Hot Springs
attractions.aka: Shakes Slough tubs, Stikine River
attractions.season: Jet-boat access May-Sep; river ice and low water limit winter
attractions.blurb: Two free tubs, one enclosed and one open-air, on a slough off the Stikine River - a standard stop on a Wrangell jet-boat day.
attractions.tip: No road, no fee, no attendant; charters from Wrangell usually combine the springs with Shakes Glacier and Shakes Lake icebergs.
attractions.duration_note: part of a full-day Stikine River trip

attractions.name: White Sulphur Springs & USFS Cabin
attractions.aka: Chichagof Island outer coast hot springs
attractions.season: Year-round; floatplane access is weather-dependent
attractions.blurb: Bathhouse built over a hot spring on Chichagof's outer coast, with a Forest Service cabin facing the open North Pacific.
attractions.tip: Reserve on recreation.gov months ahead; getting there means a floatplane from Sitka or a committing outer-coast kayak trip.

attractions.name: Goddard Hot Springs
attractions.aka: Sitka's city-owned hot tubs, Hot Springs Bay
attractions.season: Year-round; depends entirely on sea state
attractions.blurb: Two cedar tubs in open shelters 16 mi south of Sitka, maintained by the city and free to use; boat or floatplane access only.
attractions.tip: No camping at the springs and no facilities - most people fold it into a Sitka Sound charter or a rented skiff on a calm day.

attractions.name: Kenai River Personal-Use Dipnet Fishery
attractions.aka: Kenai dipnetting, North and South Beach
attractions.season: Open July 10-31 only, Alaska residents
attractions.blurb: Alaska residents net sockeye from the beaches at the mouth of the Kenai River each July - one of the wildest scenes in the state.
attractions.tip: Residents only, 25 fish for the head of household; non-residents can still watch and photograph from the North Beach lot.

attractions.name: Deep Creek Tractor-Launch Halibut Charters
attractions.aka: Deep Creek SRA, Ninilchik beach launch
attractions.season: Charters mid-May-early Sep
attractions.blurb: Charter boats get pushed into Cook Inlet by farm tractors straight off the beach here - halibut and kings with no harbor at all.
attractions.tip: Departures are tide-driven and can be 5am; Ninilchik boats usually undercut Homer for the same halibut grounds.
attractions.duration_note: full day; 3.5 hr drive from Anchorage

attractions.name: Sitka Sound Salmon & Halibut Charters
attractions.aka: Thomsen Harbor, ANB Harbor charter fleet
attractions.season: Charter season May-Sep
attractions.blurb: Sheltered island water plus quick open-Pacific access makes Sitka the most consistent all-species charter port in Southeast.
attractions.tip: King salmon peak late May-June and silvers in August; boats leave from Thomsen and ANB harbors, 10 min from downtown.

attractions.name: Clam Gulch Razor Clamming
attractions.aka: Clam Gulch State Recreation Area
attractions.season: Minus tides May-Jul, but only in years the beach is opened
attractions.blurb: Classic east-side Cook Inlet razor clam beach below a bluff campground, with Redoubt and Iliamna volcanoes across the water.
attractions.tip: East-side beaches have been closed or only briefly opened since 2015 - check ADF&G emergency orders before buying a clam shovel.
attractions.duration_note: time it to a -2 ft or lower tide

attractions.name: Homer Jackpot Halibut Derby
attractions.aka: Homer Spit halibut derby
attractions.season: Runs mid-May through mid-Sep
attractions.blurb: Season-long halibut derby on the Homer Spit; tagged-fish prizes reach six figures and the leaderboard runs all summer.
attractions.tip: Buy the ticket before you leave the dock - a 300-lb fish caught without one wins nothing. Tickets sold all over the Spit.
attractions.duration_note: add-on to any Homer charter day

attractions.name: Seward Silver Salmon Derby
attractions.aka: Resurrection Bay coho derby
attractions.season: Nine days in mid-August
attractions.blurb: Nine days each August when Resurrection Bay fills with coho and the whole town fishes; weigh-ins at the small boat harbor.
attractions.tip: Charters and beds in Seward sell out for derby week - book by spring, or fish from shore at Lowell Point for free.
attractions.duration_note: full day; the busiest week of Seward's year

attractions.name: Icy Strait Point ZipRider
attractions.aka: Hoonah Mountain zipline
attractions.season: Operates on cruise-ship days, roughly May-Sep
attractions.blurb: One of the world's longest ziplines: 5,330 ft dropping 1,300 ft off Hoonah Mountain, six riders side by side at up to 60 mph.
attractions.tip: Only worth the money on a clear day for the Icy Strait view; the gondola ride to the summit is a separate ticket.
attractions.duration_note: 2 hr of a cruise port call

attractions.name: Alaska Canopy Adventures Rainforest Zipline
attractions.aka: Bear Creek zipline, Herring Cove
attractions.season: May-Sep, aligned with the cruise season
attractions.blurb: Eight-line zip and sky-bridge course through old-growth Tongass canopy above Herring Cove, 20 min south of Ketchikan.
attractions.tip: It rains constantly and runs anyway - rain gear is issued. Pair it with the Herring Cove bear platform in August.
attractions.duration_note: 3 hr including transfer from the cruise docks

attractions.name: Crow Creek Mine
attractions.aka: Crow Creek Road gold panning, Girdwood
attractions.season: Open mid-May-mid Sep
attractions.blurb: An 1898 hydraulic gold camp above Girdwood with eight original buildings, where you rent a pan and work genuinely paying gravel.
attractions.tip: Buy the pan and stay all day in the creek; the last 3 mi of Crow Creek Road is gravel but fine for a rental car in summer.

attractions.name: Bishop's Beach Tide Pooling
attractions.aka: Homer beach walk, Kachemak Bay tidepools
attractions.season: Best on minus tides, mostly May-Jul
attractions.blurb: Homer's walk-from-town beach; a minus tide exposes acres of sea stars, anemones and octopus dens under the Kachemak bluffs.
attractions.tip: Only worth doing on a minus tide - check the Homer tide table and start an hour before low water, walking east.

attractions.name: Powerline Pass Trail
attractions.aka: Glen Alps blueberry picking, Chugach State Park
attractions.season: Snow-free Jun-Oct; skied and fat-biked in winter
attractions.blurb: Wide 11-mi valley trail from Glen Alps into Chugach State Park - moose, Dall sheep and Anchorage's best blueberry picking.
attractions.tip: Berries ripen late Jul-early Sep on the slopes past Mile 2; the Glen Alps lot fills by 10am on summer weekends.

attractions.name: K'esugi Ridge Trail
attractions.aka: Kesugi Ridge, Denali State Park traverse
attractions.season: Usually snow-free early Jul-mid Sep
attractions.blurb: 27-mi alpine ridge traverse in Denali State Park with the best unobstructed Denali views you can reach on foot.
attractions.tip: Hike southbound from Little Coal Creek for the least climbing; you need a car shuttle to Ermine Hill or Byers Lake.
attractions.duration_note: 2-3 days, or a long day out to Ermine Hill

attractions.name: Bomber Traverse & Mint Hut
attractions.aka: Mint-Bomber-Snowbird huts, Hatcher Pass
attractions.season: Late Jun-early Sep, depending on snowpack
attractions.blurb: Hatcher Pass hut-to-hut loop over three glaciers past a 1957 B-29 wreck, linking the Mint, Bomber and Snowbird huts.
attractions.tip: Huts are free and first-come from the Mountaineering Club of Alaska; the traverse needs rope, crampons and glacier skills.
attractions.duration_note: 3-4 days from the Gold Mint trailhead

attractions.name: Caines Head State Recreation Area & Fort McGilvray
attractions.aka: Lowell Point beach route, Seward
attractions.season: Beach route Jun-Sep; cabins bookable year-round
attractions.blurb: Abandoned WWII coastal gun fort on a headland south of Seward; the beach approach is walkable only near low tide.
attractions.tip: Miss the tide window and you are stuck until the next one, or paying a water taxi from Lowell Point. Two cabins bookable.
attractions.duration_note: full day; 4.5 mi each way from Lowell Point

attractions.name: Serenity Falls Hut, Eklutna Lake
attractions.aka: Eklutna Lakeside Trail hut
attractions.season: Bookable year-round; trail bikeable Jun-Oct, skiable in winter
attractions.blurb: A 13-room public hut 13 mi up the Eklutna Lakeside Trail - the largest bookable backcountry hut in Alaska, reachable by bike.
attractions.tip: The lakeside trail is flat; ride it in about 2 hrs. Reserve through Alaska State Parks and bring water treatment and a stove.

attractions.name: White Mountains NRA Public-Use Cabins
attractions.aka: Lee's Cabin, BLM White Mountains winter trails
attractions.season: Trails groomed roughly Dec-Mar; wet and boggy in summer
attractions.blurb: BLM's 12-cabin winter trail network north of Fairbanks; Lee's Cabin, 7 mi in, is the standard first ski or fat-bike overnight.
attractions.tip: Cabins are $25 and book 30 days out on recreation.gov; trails are only groomed once snow is deep, so avoid Nov and April.
attractions.duration_note: overnight; 7 mi from the Wickersham Dome trailhead

attractions.name: Homer Harbor Water Taxi
attractions.aka: Kachemak Bay water taxi, Homer Spit ramp
attractions.season: Scheduled daily May-Sep; on-call the rest of the year
attractions.blurb: The only practical way into Kachemak Bay State Park - skiffs run from the Homer Spit to Grewingk, Halibut Cove and Tutka Bay.
attractions.tip: Book the return leg too and agree a hard pickup time and beach; weather regularly shifts the afternoon runs.

attractions.name: Grewingk Glacier Hand Tram
attractions.aka: Kachemak Bay State Park cable tram, Grewingk Creek
attractions.season: Trail snow-free Jun-Sep; tram serviced in summer
attractions.blurb: Hand-cranked cable tram over Grewingk Creek in Kachemak Bay State Park - the crux of the Saddle-to-Glacier-Spit loop.
attractions.tip: It takes two people to pull; the alternative is a cold thigh-deep ford. Water taxi in at Saddle Trail, out at Glacier Spit.
attractions.duration_note: full day from Homer including boat

attractions.name: Swan Lake Canoe Route
attractions.aka: Kenai NWR canoe system, Swan Lake Road
attractions.season: Ice-free roughly late May-Sep
attractions.blurb: Kenai Refuge canoe route linking 30 lakes and the Moose River by short portages - Alaska's most accessible multi-day paddle.
attractions.tip: Put in at Portage Lake off Swan Lake Road; the longer 80-mi Swanson River route next door sees a fraction of the traffic.

attractions.name: Lynx Lake Canoe Route
attractions.aka: Nancy Lake canoe trail, Tanaina Lake trailhead
attractions.season: Ice-free Jun-Sep; skied and skated in winter
attractions.blurb: Marked canoe loop through 14 lakes in Nancy Lake State Rec Area, with public-use cabins you paddle straight up to.
attractions.tip: Cabins book six months out via Alaska State Parks; flat water and short portages make this a strong first canoe-camping trip.

attractions.name: Devil's Canyon Jet Boat
attractions.aka: Susitna River jet boat, Talkeetna
attractions.season: Runs late May-mid Sep, water-level dependent
attractions.blurb: Jet boat runs 50 mi up the Susitna from Talkeetna into the whitewater gorge at Devil's Canyon, with a trapper-cabin stop.
attractions.tip: Trips leave Talkeetna mid-morning; it is the only way to see the canyon short of a Class VI kayak expedition.

attractions.name: Pioneer Peak Trail
attractions.aka: Knik River Pioneer Ridge
attractions.season: Snow-free Jun-Sep; icy and avalanche-prone in shoulder season
attractions.blurb: Brutally steep 6-mi trail climbing 5,300 ft straight off the Knik River flats to the south knob above Palmer.
attractions.tip: Roughly 1,000 ft of gain per mile with no water on route; stop at the 5,300-ft knob unless you can scramble exposed rock.

attractions.name: Manitoba Cabin
attractions.aka: Alaska Huts, Seward Highway Mile 49
attractions.season: Bookable year-round; best on snow Jan-Mar
attractions.blurb: Bookable Alaska Huts cabin at Mile 49 of the Seward Highway - wood stove, bunks, and ski and bike trails out the door.
attractions.tip: It sits only a few hundred yards off the highway, so it works as a soft intro to hut trips; bring food and a sleeping bag.

attractions.name: Kasilof River Personal-Use Dipnet
attractions.aka: Kasilof dipnetting, Cook Inlet
attractions.season: Opens late June and runs into early August, Alaska residents only
attractions.blurb: The other Cook Inlet dipnet fishery: opens two weeks before the Kenai and can be worked from shore or a skiff at the mouth.
attractions.tip: Alaska residents only, and the beach is soft sand - bring 4WD or you will dig out. Far less chaotic than Kenai's North Beach.

attractions.name: Nizina River Packraft Float
attractions.aka: McCarthy packrafting, Nizina to Chitina River
attractions.season: Jun-early Sep; flows peak in July heat
attractions.blurb: The classic Wrangell-St. Elias packraft: shuttle out of McCarthy to the Nizina, then float glacial braids toward the Chitina.
attractions.tip: Two to three days of Class II braided water with no rescue; most parties fly one leg with a McCarthy air taxi.

attractions.name: Arctic Valley Ski Area
attractions.aka: Alpenglow at Arctic Valley, Ship Creek ridge
attractions.season: Lifts spin weekends Dec-Mar; access road open Jun-Sep
attractions.blurb: Anchorage's non-profit ski hill above Ship Creek - two chairs in winter, and the city's best blueberry slopes in August.
attractions.tip: The 7-mi gravel road up is fine for a rental car in summer; berry picking above the lodge peaks mid-to-late August.

attractions.name: Johnson Pass Trail
attractions.aka: Historic Iditarod Trail, Granite Creek to Moose Pass
attractions.season: Snow-free July-Sep; skied and fat-biked in winter
attractions.blurb: 23-mi gold-rush-era trail over a low pass between Granite Creek and Moose Pass - the Kenai's best long trail run or bike.
attractions.tip: Both trailheads sit on the Seward Highway so a car shuttle is easy; snow lingers at the pass into early July.
attractions.duration_note: long day by bike, 2 days on foot
